text:
On Monday night, Team USA's men's gymnastics team earned their first medal 芒聙聰 a third-place bronze 芒聙聰 for the first time since George Bush was in the Oval Office. While the long-awaited medal moment spoke to the combined talents of the five-person team, it was Pommel Horse Guy's efforts that propelled them to the podium.脗

Unlike his teammates, Stephen Nedoroscik was the only member of the group to qualify for a single event at the Olympics, the pommel horse, a notoriously difficult event that requires athletes to continuously balance and swing their bodies atop an apparatus with two handles using only their hands (and impressive core strength) to support themselves.脗

Stephen Nedoroscik had to score big on pommel horse for Team USA in the last rotation芒聙娄

AND HE DID. 冒聼陇炉

冒聼聯潞: NBC and Peacockpic.twitter.com/hkhiHpovyh

芒聙聰 NBC Olympics & Paralympics (@NBCOlympics)July 29, 2024



Related

The pommel horse wasn't the only place Nedoroscik gained momentum, however. The 25-year-old quickly found major internet appeal where social media users and Olympic fans homed in on his pre- and post-competition antics, which largely included dozing off on the sidelines until his event turn. "OUR ROMAN EMPIRE," wrote NBC Olympics' official Instagram account, alongsidean imageof a seemingly snoozing (or is it meditating?) Nedoroscik, a reference to theinternet trendthat went viral in 2023.

Here are a few things you should know about the internet's latest Olympic obsession.

Originally from Worcester, Massachusetts, Nedoroscik studied electrical engineering at Penn State University, where he also competed in gymnastics. His profession 芒聙聰 and bespectacled appearance 芒聙聰 earned him the title of "nerdy" from fans across social media.脗

脗

After being named to the Olympic team in June, Nederoscik told press that it had long been on his radar to qualify for the Games. 芒聙聹When I was very, very young people would tell me, 芒聙聵One day you芒聙聶re going to be an Olympian!芒聙聶 芒聙聺 he said,according to NPR.芒聙聹Back then I was just a dorky little kid. And now look at me 芒聙聰 I芒聙聶m a dorky adult, going to the Olympics.芒聙聺

脗

AuthorJohn Greenchimed inon X/Twitter,happy to report that "Stephen Nedoroscik just struck a blow for nerds everywhere. Beautiful."

脗

"People who aren't nerds don't understand how long we've been waiting for an electrical engineer from Pennsylvania to [sic] POMMEL THE HELL OUT OF A HORSE," Green added ina separate tweet.

脗

To hear it in Green's own words, "To truly understand Stephen Nedoroscik's nerd credentials, you need to know that he is in Paris for the Olympics and posting to his insta story aboutsolving a Rubik's cubein under 10 seconds."

脗

To truly understand Stephen Nedoroscik's nerd credentials, you need to know that he is in Paris for the Olympics and posting to his insta story about solving a rubik's cube in under 10 seconds.pic.twitter.com/2vFygJBifW

芒聙聰 John Green (@sportswithjohn)July 29, 2024



脗

Nedoroscik, a seeming Rubik's cube aficionado, took to his Instagram account ahead of his team competition to get some cubing time in. 芒聙聹It芒聙聶s stress relief,芒聙聺 he said told TODAY on Tuesday. 芒聙聹Sometimes I make the excuse that it芒聙聶s good for wrist rehab, too.芒聙聺

脗

CNN reportedthat Nedoroscik's personal record for solving a Rubik's cube is 8.6 seconds. The outlet also noted that he dabbles in other forms of puzzling, as well. After a gymnast friend put him on to "killer sudoku,"脗聽 Nedoroscik became the 43rd person in the world to solve a sudoku puzzle that claimed to be the "world's hardest."

脗

It's a bird! It's a plane! It's Pommel Horse Guy!

脗

Nedoroscik was quickly dubbed the "Clark Kent" of the Games, a reference toSuperman'salter ego with a lackluster bespectacled disguise. Clips of the athlete ostensibly napping during other apparatus events took place went viral. Once it was his turn, he whipped off his glasses to demonstrate his mastery of the skill, secured the medal and returned to the sidelines.脗聽脗

脗

"I'm a goofy guy with glasses on, but as soon as I take them off I'm locked in," Nedorosciktold TODAY.

脗

https://www.instagram.com/p/C-DB3VbR6Zp/?hl=en

脗

Separate from super-hero coded references, the internet also found a way to liken Nedoroscik toKen,whose job in the"Barbie" moviewas infamously just "beach." Given that the Olympian quite literally had one job to perform, it's an apt comparison.脗

脗

Obsessed with this guy on the US men's gymnastics team who's only job is pommel horse, so he just sits there until he's activated like a sleeper agent, whips off his glasses like Clark Kent and does a pommel horse routine that helps deliver the team its first medal in 16 years.pic.twitter.com/0D1ZqJjFa1

芒聙聰 Megan 冒聼聯職 (@MegWritesBooks)July 29, 2024



脗

脗

While speaking on theTODAY broadcast,Nedoroscik tugged on his ear, a special movement he shared was a signal between him and his grandfather, who passed away last year. His 芒聙聹dziadziu," (which means "grandfather" in Polish) served in World War II and was Nedoroscik's "idol."

脗

芒聙聹I芒聙聶ve kept the tradition up, and now it always means, 芒聙聵Hey,芒聙聶 to everyone that I love,芒聙聺 the gymnast said. 芒聙聹When I was at Penn State, that was the thing that I did whenever I was on TV to say, 芒聙聵Hey, Dziadziu.芒聙聶芒聙聺

脗

https://www.instagram.com/p/C-C98P7xosx/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=embed_video_watch_again

脗

Tess McCracken, Nedoroscik's long-term girlfriend of eight years, was present to watch the moment her boyfriend became a viral sensation and Olympic medalist all in one. 芒聙聹I was so lightheaded from screaming that I almost fainted,芒聙聺 the 26-year-old told NBC in a phone interview.脗

脗

Stephen Nedoroscik, you are an OLYMPIAN芒聺陇茂赂聫冒聼聡潞冒聼聡赂pic.twitter.com/YNN0Q0W3dT

芒聙聰 Tess McCracken (@tess_mccracken)July 1, 2024



脗

McCracken, who met Nedoroscik at Penn State, described him as 芒聙聹goofy guy芒聙聺 who is 芒聙聹really lighthearted芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹always sees the bright side of things.芒聙聺

脗

芒聙聹Blowing up on the internet has been such an unexpected side effect of this whole adventure,芒聙聺 she added. 芒聙聹I have people like tagging me, calling me 芒聙聵Lois Lane.芒聙聶 I芒聙聶m like, 芒聙聵OK, I芒聙聶m not going to be mad about it.芒聙聶芒聙聺
